headline: Israel is using the ceasefire in Gaza to cement its grip on the territory
description: Nearly eight months after Israel announced a ceasefire that its army is violating daily, the country continues to strike the part of the enclave it occupies. Some in Israel are calling to resume airstrikes more intensively.

Cuba questions 'seriousness' of US approach to talks
description: Relations between Havana and Washington – which were already fraught following the imposition of a US energy blockade on the island in January – have significantly worsened with the indictment of former president Raul Castro by a Florida court last week.

headline: Mistral AI aims for 'full value-chain presence' to challenge US dominance
description: The French artificial intelligence leader continues to expand its data center network and highlights partnerships with major European industrial groups in an effort to narrow the gap with the sector's dominant American players.

headline: Bruce Springsteen calls out the White House and announces a protest festival: 'Join us and let's fight for the America that we love'
description: Springsteen, a vocal critic of US President Donald Trump, will headline a star-studded protest along with Foo Fighters, Dave Matthews, Brittany Howard and Joan Baez at a protest festival in Washington, DC, a month before the midterm elections. 'There is no one coming to save us. We've got to do it ourselves,' he said on Wednesday.

headline: Why German trains are never on time anymore
description: The German rail network has deteriorated over the past 20 years, to the point that Deutsche Bahn has become a source of national embarrassment.

How France's early heatwave is affecting plants and wildlife
description: Birds that nest under rooftops, such as swifts and swallows, are particularly at risk during heatwaves. This unprecedented spell is taking place right in the middle of the nesting season, but it is not accompanied by drought conditions.

headline: Revealed: KTM's secretly derestricted and highly polluting motorcycles
description: The Austrian manufacturer KTM is facilitating the systematic removal of speed and power restrictions from some of its motorcycles in France and across Europe, making them illegal and highly polluting, according to the investigation 'Unrestricted' by a media consortium including Le Monde.

headline: 'Do you realize that your statements are completely absurd?': Inside the arrest of the Louvre burglars
description: In their new book, journalists Jean-Michel Décugis, Nicolas-Charles Torrent and Le Monde's Jérémie Pham-Lê recount the events of the burglary at the Paris museum on October 19, 2025, and the arrest of the suspects. Here are excerpts from the book.
byline: Jean-Michel Décugis,  Jérémie Pham-Lê and  Nicolas-Charles Torrent
